event: The Prodigal Son in modern dress -- a parlor gathering
symbols: shared table — fellowship and communion, the table as a place of encounter (cf. the Last Supper, the Emmaus meal)
standing figure looking outward — restlessness or contemplation, perhaps the prodigal pondering departure or return
window light filtering in — divine light entering the mundane, the possibility of revelation in ordinary life
flowers in vase — beauty and transience amid the domestic scene
bay window framing the outside world — the boundary between the enclosed domestic space and the larger world of temptation or opportunity
description: An interior scene depicting four men gathered around a large wooden table in a well-appointed Victorian parlor. Three men are seated: one at the far left appears young and holds something near his face, the second leans back casually, and the third at the far right is an older man who sits quietly. The fourth figure stands at center, silhouetted against a large bay window through which diffused light enters, casting the room in warm, muted tones. A tea or coffee service, cups, and a small vase of flowers are arranged on the table.
